plan: Step 1: Audit existing test coverage against acceptance criteria — Read test files in tests/us-006/, us-007/, us-008/, us-009/ (signal collectors), tests/us-014/, us-015/, us-022/ (scoring), tests/us-024/, us-025/ (inference), tests/us-011/, us-012/, us-013/ (receipt render) to confirm they satisfy AC 1, 2, 3, and 5. Document any gaps. No new test files are created for already-covered areas.

Step 2: Verify signal collector coverage (AC 1) — Confirm tests/us-009/snapshot.test.ts covers collectSnapshot() return shape, browser API mocking, sentinel values, immutability, and type exports. Confirm tests/us-006/ (locale), us-007/ (device), us-008/ (rendering) cover individual signal collectors. If all pass via `npm run test`, mark AC 1 as satisfied by existing tests.

Step 3: Verify scoring coverage (AC 2) — Confirm tests/us-014/entropy-score.test.ts covers entropy scoring (weights, boundaries, edge cases) and tests/us-015/privacy-posture.test.ts covers privacy posture classification. Confirm tests/us-022/permission-debt-score.test.ts covers permission debt scoring. If all pass, mark AC 2 as satisfied by existing tests.

Step 4: Verify inference coverage (AC 3) — Confirm tests/us-024/inference-schema.test.ts covers schema validation and tests/us-025/inference-rules.test.ts covers all 5 inference rules plus applyInferenceRules() integration. If all pass, mark AC 3 as satisfied by existing tests.

Step 5: Create main page smoke test (AC 4) — Create tests/us-034/main-smoke.test.ts using jsdom environment. Test the bootstrap flow in src/app/main.ts: mock renderIntro (async), renderShell, renderZeroClickOsintModule, and renderPermissionDebtModule (async). Verify: (a) app element is created and passed to renderIntro, (b) renderShell is called after intro completes, (c) dashboard modules are appended in correct order, (d) no errors thrown during bootstrap. Use vi.mock() for module imports since DOM rendering requires browser APIs.

Step 6: Verify receipt render smoke coverage (AC 5) — Confirm tests/us-012/fingerprint-receipt.test.ts and tests/us-013/format-snapshot.test.ts cover receipt rendering with formatted snapshot data, row counts, label/value structure. Confirm tests/us-011/receipt.test.ts covers receipt component rendering, CSS structure, and design tokens. If existing tests provide adequate smoke-level coverage, mark AC 5 as satisfied. Only add a thin integration test in tests/us-034/ if a gap is found (e.g., end-to-end receipt rendering from raw snapshot to DOM is not tested).

Step 7: Create coverage manifest — Add tests/us-034/coverage-manifest.test.ts that programmatically verifies critical test files exist and are not excluded by the package.json exclusion pattern. This ensures signal, scoring, inference, and receipt tests remain in the test suite and are not accidentally skipped (addresses the us-001/us-002 exclusion pattern concern).

Step 8: Defer Playwright E2E to a separate ticket — Playwright is not currently installed (~200MB browser download), no playwright.config.ts exists, and the CI/deployment pipeline (static hosting) has not been validated for Playwright support. Instead of adding Playwright in this ticket, document the recommendation and create a follow-up ticket. The main page smoke test (Step 5) provides equivalent smoke coverage at the unit level using jsdom.
Step 9: Run npm run lint, npm run typecheck, npm run test to validate all existing and new tests pass. Verify no tests/us-034/ files are caught by the existing exclusion patterns in package.json.
testing_recommendations: Run `npm run test` to confirm all 43+ existing test files pass, including the new tests/us-034/ files
Verify tests/us-034/ is not matched by the package.json exclusion patterns (currently only us-001 and us-002/lint are excluded)
Run `npm run lint` and `npm run typecheck` to ensure new test files meet project standards
Manually verify AC satisfaction: grep test descriptions in us-006 through us-009 for signal coverage, us-014/us-015/us-022 for scoring, us-024/us-025 for inference
Confirm the main page smoke test correctly mocks all async functions (renderIntro, renderPermissionDebtModule) and tests the bootstrap sequence
risks: Existing tests may have subtle gaps not visible from file-level inspection — need to run them and verify pass status before claiming AC satisfaction
The main page smoke test depends on mocking 4 module imports; if src/app/main.ts has side effects at import time (e.g., immediate DOM access), jsdom setup may need careful ordering
The coverage manifest test (Step 7) creates a coupling to file paths — if test directories are reorganized, this test will need updating
Deferring Playwright means no true browser E2E smoke test exists yet; jsdom-based tests may miss rendering or browser-API issues that only surface in real browsers
